item.title: 编程是权衡的艺术 | Programming is the art of trade-off
item.description:
无论您使用哪种编程语言，您都可能听到的一个常见建议是：

不要使用 switch 语句

除了人们通常忘记添加 break 语句外，更深层次的原因是开发人员经常避免在代码中使用特殊情况。 相反，他们更喜欢使用更灵活和更强大的结构，例如多态或字典。

我听到许多有经验的开发人员说他们将代码视为艺术。 这并不难理解，因为他们会花费数小时甚至数天的时间来处理一段特定的代码。 因此，他们将代码视为个人风格和审美偏好的反映，就像创作艺术品的艺术家一样。

然而，这种对优雅、设计良好的代码的追求有时会导致对特殊情况处理的忽视。 创造完美代码的愿望很容易让人冲昏头脑，但这种痴迷有时会使开发人员看不到他们试图解决的真正问题。 换句话说，这种对完美代码的执着就像白纸上的污点，模糊了大局。

特例一

在我之前的公司，我们将我们的产品从一个网络数据分析平台更新为一个完整的营销平台。 支持此升级的主要功能是在引擎盖下添加 CDP（客户数据平台）。 因此，您可以创建用户细分并在他们访问您的网站时为他们制作个性化内容：

如何实现？ 从技术上讲，我们将从网站收集的所有用户行为数据存储在 MongoDB 中。 当访客来访时，我们会尝试根据他的身份、我们的 SDK 为匿名用户生成的 cookie ID 或通过登录等跟踪事件识别的真实用户 ID 来查看他是否可以匹配特定的用户细分。

启动一段时间后，我们发现分段匹配过程越来越慢。 事实证明，主要原因是存储在 MongoDB 中的数据比我们预期的要多得多，这大大增加了读取延迟。

有一个众所周知的事实，高达 98% 的登陆企业网站的潜在客户都是完全匿名的：

检查我们的数据库后，它几乎是相同的数字。 如果是这样，在现实世界中，大多数匿名用户可能再也不会访问该网站，为什么我们还需要永远保存他们的行为数据？ 我与后端团队的负责人分享了我的想法，我们开始讨论可能的解决方案。

  • 他：如果你要删除一个匿名用户的数据，你怎么保证他再也不会回来了？
  • 我：嗯，我不能。 但我们可以猜测，根据某种启发式方法，他很有可能永远不会回来。 例如，如果一个匿名用户的最后一次活动已经是 7 天前，这是 Safari 的 cookie 生命周期。
  • 他：如果7天后有一天他再来怎么办？
  • 我：嗯，所以我们需要想办法恢复他的行为数据。 我们创建一个单独的冷数据库来存储所有已删除的匿名用户数据怎么样？ 对于访问网站的匿名用户，我们可以先检查是否可以在普通数据库中找到他的数据，如果不能，那么我们尝试从冷数据库中找到他的数据，并通过从冷数据库中移动来对数据进行反应。数据库到普通数据库。
  • 他：听起来像是一个解决方案。 但是我们需要为这种特殊情况编写额外的代码。 而且如果发生这种情况，由于额外的数据库查询，它会变得比现在更慢。
  • 我：是的，这是我们需要处理的特殊情况，但它可以解决问题。 由于这是一个特例，让我们看看它发生的频率。

修改后问题解决，根据log，很少出现这种特殊情况。

特例 2

我和我的联合创始人正在 Prisma 之上构建全栈工具包ZenStack 。 我们的一位客户询问我们是否可以解决未解决的 Prisma 问题：

添加 findManyAndCount 以返回查询项目的计数 #7550

不知道为什么 Prisma 没有提供它，但它似乎是一个很容易实现的包装器：

 const { items, count } = await prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => { const items = await tx.user.findMany(query); const count = await tx.user.count({ where: query.where }); return { items, count }; });

显然，它需要为每个 API 调用执行两次查询。 但这是基本的数据库限制，似乎我们无能为力，对吧？

我们的一位客户不这么认为，他提供了另一种特殊情况处理如下：

 const { items, count } = await prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => { const items = await tx.user.findMany(query); // If it is true, we could avoid another query if (query.take && items.length < query.take) { return { items, count: items.length }; } const count = await tx.user.count({ where: query.where }); return { items, count }; });

这是他的引述：

特别是在构建管理仪表板时，如果对数据库使用进行计量，则每次更改过滤器或表上的排序方向时都会增加额外的计数，因此如果确实很棒的 ZenStack 解决方案可以将其考虑在内

结论

由于许多人认为编程与数学有很强的关系。 他们通常会尝试找到适合所有情况的单一解决方案，例如数学公式。 然而，编程不是编写可运行的代码，而是为现实世界的问题找到有效的解决方案。 现实世界的问题通常是复杂的和多方面的，因此，它们通常没有放之四海而皆准的方法。

我确实相信编程是一门艺术，但它是一门权衡的艺术。 它是时间和空间、稳定性和灵活性、性能和代码复杂性等之间的权衡。平衡这些权衡的能力是编程的重要组成部分，也是使编程成为一门艺术的原因。

因此，只要您知道这是正确的权衡，就不要害怕做出特殊情况并为此编写更多代码。 那是艺术的一部分。

PS, We're building ZenStack , a toolkit that supercharges Prisma ORM with a powerful access control layer and unleashes its full potential for full-stack development. PS，我们正在构建ZenStack ，这是一个通过强大的访问控制层增强 Prisma ORM 并释放其全栈开发潜力的工具包。



原文:

No matter what programming language you are using, one common suggestion you all probably hear is that:

Don’t use switch statements

Besides people usually forgetting to add the break statement, the more profound reason is that developers often avoid using special cases in their code. Instead, they prefer to use more flexible and powerful constructs such as polymorphism or dictionaries.

I heard lots of experienced developers say that they view their code as art. It’s not hard to understand since they spend hours or even days working on a particular piece of code. Therefore they view their code as a reflection of their personal style and aesthetic preferences, much like artists who create works of art.

However, this pursuit of elegant, well-designed code can sometimes lead to a disregard for special case handling. It's easy to get carried away with the desire to create perfect code, but this obsession can sometimes blind developers from seeing the real problems they are trying to solve. In other words, this fixation on perfect code can be like a stain on white paper, obscuring the bigger picture.

Special Case 1

In my previous company, we updated our product from a web data analytics platform to a complete marketing platform. The major feature supporting this upgrade is to add a CDP(Customer Data Platform) under the hood. Therefore, you can create a user segment and make personalized content for them when they visit your website:

How to achieve that? Technically we store all the user behavior data collected from the website in a MongoDB. When a visitor comes, we will try to see whether he could match a particular user segment based on his identity, a cookie ID generated by our SDK for the anonymous user, or a real user ID identified by the tracking event like login.

After launching for a while, we found that the segment-matching process becomes slower and slower. It turns out that the main reason is that the data stored in MongoDB are way more than we expected which increases the read latency dramatically.

There is a very well-known fact that up to 98% of potential customers landing on corporate websites are completely anonymous:

After checking our database, it’s almost the same number. If so, in the real world, most anonymous users probably would never visit the website again, why do we still need to hold their behavior data forever? I shared my idea with the leader of the backend team and we commenced discussions on potential solutions.

  • He: If you want to remove an anonymous user’s data, how could you make sure he will never come back again?
  • Me: Well, I couldn’t. But we could guess there is a big chance he will never return based on some heuristic method. For example, if one anonymous user’s last activity was already 7 days ago, which is the cookie lifetime for Safari.
  • He: What if he comes again one day after 7 days?
  • Me: Hm, so we need to find a way to recover his behavior data. How about we create a separate cold database to store all the deleted anonymous user data? For an anonymous user who comes to the website, we could first check whether we could find his data in the normal database, if not, then we try to find his data from the cold database, and reactive the data by moving it from the cold database to the normal database.
  • He: It sounds like a solution. But we need to write additional code for this special case. And also if it happens, it will become even slower than now because of an extra database query.
  • Me: Yes, it’s a special case that we need to handle, but it could solve the problem. And since it’s a special case, let’s see how often it could happen.

After making the change, the problem is solved, and according to the log, the special case rarely happens.

Special Case 2

My co-founder and I are building the full-stack toolkit ZenStack on top of Prisma. One of our customers asked if we could resolve an unresolved Prisma issue:

Add findManyAndCount to return count of queried items #7550

Don’t know why Prisma didn’t provide that, but it seems like an easy wrapper to achieve so:

const { items, count } = await prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => { const items = await tx.user.findMany(query); const count = await tx.user.count({ where: query.where }); return { items, count };
});

Obviously, it requires two queries for each API call. But that’s the fundamental database limitation, it seems nothing we can do, right?

One of our customers doesn’t think so, he offered another special case handling as below:

const { items, count } = await prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => { const items = await tx.user.findMany(query); // If it is true, we could avoid another query if (query.take && items.length < query.take) { return { items, count: items.length }; } const count = await tx.user.count({ where: query.where }); return { items, count };
});

Here is his quote:

Especially when building an admin dashboard, extra counts every time you change a filter or a sort direction on a table add up if your database usage is metered, so if the assuredly awesome ZenStack solution could factor that in

Conclusion

Since many people consider programming to have a strong relationship with mathematics. They usually try to find a single solution that fits all cases, like a mathematical formula. However, programming is not about writing code that runs, it is about finding effective solutions to real-world problems. Real-world problems are often complex and multifaceted, and as such, they typically do not have a one-size-fits-all approach.

I do believe programming is an art, but it’s an art of trade-off. It’s a trade-off between time and space, stability and flexibility, performance and code complexity, etc. The ability to balance these trade-offs is an essential part of programming and is what makes it an art.

Therefore, as long as you know it’s the right trade-off, don’t be afraid to make the special case and write more code for that. That’s part of the art.

P.S., We're building ZenStack, a toolkit that supercharges Prisma ORM with a powerful access control layer and unleashes its full potential for full-stack development.
item.author: jiasheng

Once suspended, emmanuel_onyeike will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, emmanuel_onyeike will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by emmanuel_onyeike will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

Make all posts by emmanuel_onyeike less visible

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging emmanuel_onyeike will restore default visibility to their posts.

item.title: 解决Python报错：subprocess-exited-with-error | Solve Python error: subprocess-exited-with-error
item.description:
如果您是 Python 开发人员，您可能在某个时候遇到过子进程错误。 最常见的子流程错误之一是“subprocess-exited-with-error”。 当在您的 Python 代码中运行的子进程因错误退出时会发生此错误。 错误消息通常包括有关子流程和错误代码的信息。

本文将为您提供一些提示和技术，帮助您解决 Python 中的“subprocess-exited-with-error”错误。 我们将讨论此错误的常见原因、如何诊断它以及如何修复它。

是什么原因导致“子进程因错误而退出”？

通常，“subprocess-exited-with-error”发生在 Python 生成的进程以错误代码结束时。 这可能由于多种原因而发生，例如：

  • 传递给子进程的参数或自变量不正确。
  • 权限不足或缺乏访问某些资源的权限。
  • 子进程正在执行的程序中的错误。
  • 正在执行的程序中的语法或运行时错误。
  • 子进程在其中运行的环境变量存在问题。

诊断“subprocess-exited-with-error”

要诊断此错误，您需要先检查错误消息。 错误消息通常显示在控制台上，它包含有关错误代码、子进程和正在执行的命令的信息。 错误消息还可能包含有关传递给进程的参数的信息。

检查完错误消息后，您就可以开始调试代码了。 您可能希望使用调试器单步执行代码并确定导致错误的任何问题。

修复“subprocess-exited-with-error”

您可以采取几个步骤来修复“subprocess-exited-with-error”。 以下是一些可以帮助您解决此问题的提示和技巧：

检查你的参数和参数

确保您将正确的参数和参数传递给子流程。 如果子流程需要一个参数，请确保您以正确的格式传递它。

检查文件权限

如果子进程正在访问需要管理权限的文件或资源，如果它没有适当的权限，它可能会失败。 确保子进程具有访问所需文件和资源的必要权限。

检查语法和运行时错误

如果错误代码与语法或运行时错误有关，则需要检查子进程正在运行的程序。 检查可能导致代码失败的语法错误、错位字符或其他问题。

检查您的环境变量

如果错误代码与环境变量有关，则需要检查传递给子流程的变量。 确保变量与运行子流程的环境一致。

使用调试工具

如果您在解决“subprocess-exited-with-error”时遇到困难，您可能需要使用调试工具来帮助您确定问题。 您可以单步执行代码以确定可能导致子流程失败的问题。

结论

通过遵循本文中概述的提示和技术，您应该能够快速解决此错误并重新开始编写出色的 Python 代码。

请记住检查您的参数和参数，检查您的环境变量，并在需要时使用调试工具。 稍加坚持和努力，您就可以解决“subprocess-exited-with-error”问题并继续构建出色的 Python 应用程序。



原文:

If you're a Python developer, you may have encountered a subprocess error at some point. One of the most common subprocess errors is "subprocess-exited-with-error." This error occurs when a subprocess running in your Python code exits with an error. The error message usually includes information about the subprocess and the error code.

This article will provide you with some tips and techniques to help you resolve "subprocess-exited-with-error" errors in Python. We'll discuss the common causes of this error, how to diagnose it, and how to fix it.

What Causes "subprocess-exited-with-error"?

Usually, "subprocess-exited-with-error" occurs when a process that has been spawned by Python ends with an error code. This may happen due to a number of reasons such as:

  • Incorrect parameters or arguments passed to the subprocess.
  • Insufficient permissions or lack of privileges to access certain resources.
  • A bug in the program being executed by the subprocess.
  • A syntax or runtime error in the program being executed.
  • An issue with the environment variables that the subprocess is running in.

Diagnosing "subprocess-exited-with-error"

To diagnose this error, you'll need to start by examining the error message. The error message is usually displayed on the console, and it contains information about the error code, the subprocess, and the command that was being executed. The error message may also contain information about the arguments that were passed to the process.

Once you've examined the error message, you can start debugging your code. You may want to use a debugger to step through your code and identify any issues that are causing the error.

Fixing "subprocess-exited-with-error"

There are several steps you can take to fix "subprocess-exited-with-error." Here are some tips and techniques that may help you resolve this issue:

Check Your Arguments and Parameters

One of the most common causes of "subprocess-exited-with-error" is incorrect arguments or parameters passed to the subprocess. Make sure that you're passing the correct arguments and parameters to the subprocess. If the subprocess is expecting a parameter, make sure that you're passing it in the correct format.

Check File Permissions

If the subprocess is accessing files or resources that require administrative privileges, it may fail if it doesn't have the proper permissions. Make sure that the subprocess has the necessary permissions to access the files and resources it needs.

Check for Syntax and Runtime Errors

If the error code is related to syntax or runtime errors, you'll need to review the program being run by the subprocess. Check for syntax errors, misplaced characters, or other issues that may be causing the code to fail.

Review Your Environment Variables

If the error code is related to environment variables, you'll need to check the variables being passed to the subprocess. Make sure that the variables are consistent with the environment in which the subprocess is being run.

Use a Debugging Tool

If you're having difficulty resolving "subprocess-exited-with-error," you may want to use a debugging tool to help you identify the issue. You can step through your code to identify issues that may be causing the subprocess to fail.

Conclusion

"Subprocess-exited-with-error" can be a frustrating error to deal with, but the good news is that it's usually easy to diagnose and fix. By following the tips and techniques outlined in this article, you should be able to resolve this error quickly and get back to writing great Python code.

Remember to check your arguments and parameters, review your environment variables, and use a debugging tool if needed. With a little persistence and effort, you can solve "subprocess-exited-with-error" and continue building awesome Python applications.
item.author: coder9

江乐寿

原文:

Giang Lê Thị

Posted on Apr 1

Đặt Tiệc Việt là đơn vị cung cấp dịch vụ nấu tiệc chuyên nghiệp và tận tâm nhất hiện nay. Với đội ngũ nhân viên được đào tạo kỹ lưỡng, Đặt Tiệc Việt cam kết mang đến cho khách hàng những trải nghiệm tuyệt vời nhất trong bữa tiệc của mình. Bên cạnh đó, công ty luôn lựa chọn nguyên liệu tươi ngon và chất lượng cao để chế biến các món ăn, đảm bảo độ ngon và an toàn thực phẩm cho thực khách.

Xem dịch vụ đặt tiệc sinh nhật: dattiecviet.vn/dat-tiec-sinh-nhat/

Adeniji Adefisayo - Mar 31

Mohtasham Sayeed Mohiuddin - Mar 31

Once unsuspended, dattiecviet will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by dattiecviet will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If dattiecviet is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

item.title: 如何在 Deta 空间上部署 FastAPI 应用程序 | How to Deploy a FastAPI App on Deta Space
item.description:
根据这里的文档，

如果您想了解更多信息，可以访问文档。 在本文中，我们关注如何在Deta Space上部署您的 FastAPI 应用程序，以便它可供世界各地的任何人使用。

关于德塔的一点。 如果你熟悉 DETA，你会知道它们曾经是 deta.sh（已弃用），但在 2 年多之后，他们决定升级到 DETA CLOUD（甚至更酷），为开发人员提供免费和简单的方式让他们的想法在互联网上传播。

我记得那时，我曾经在 DETA MICRO 上部署我的 fastAPI 应用程序（已弃用），但由于它现在已弃用，在本文中，您将学习如何在 DETA SPACE 上部署您的 FastAPI 应用程序，以便它可以用于世界各地的任何人。

但首先，让我们创建一个简单的 FastAPI 应用程序用于演示目的。

要求。

  • 注意：如果您之前有 deta.sh 帐户，您仍然可以使用该帐户登录 Deta Space。

创建一个简单的应用程序。

让我们创建一个简单的 FastAPI 应用程序。

创建一个目录。

打开一个新的终端窗口并为您的应用程序创建一个目录，我将我的命名为fastapi-tutorial ，然后cd进入它。

 $ mkdir fastapi-tutorial $ cd fastapi-tutorial

创建一个虚拟环境。

此步骤是可选的，但很有意义。 创建虚拟环境的重要性在于将您的项目依赖项与 PC 上的全局依赖项隔离开来。 对于窗户；

 $ python -m venv my-env $ my-env\Scripts\activate

或者在 Mac PC 上。

 $ python3 -m venv my-env $ Source my-env\bin\activate

安装 FastApi。

现在，您需要安装fastapi和uvicorn 。 创建一个简单的路线。

创建一个文件main.py并添加以下代码；

 from fastapi import FastAPI app = FastAPI() @app.get("/") def read_root(): return {"message": "Deploying FastApi to Deta Space"}

创建 requirements.txt 文件。

创建一个requirements.txt文件，这将包含您所有的依赖项，以便 Deta 知道要为您的应用程序安装哪个依赖项。

 (my-env) $ pip freeze > requirements.txt

打开文件，它看起来应该是这样的；

 fastapi==0.89.1 uvicorn==0.20.0

运行应用程序。

现在，运行该应用程序以确保一切正常。

 (my-env) $ uvicorn main:app --reload INFO: Uvicorn running on http://127.0.0.1:8000 (Press CTRL+C to quit) INFO: Started reloader process [28720] INFO: Started server process [28722] INFO: Waiting for application startup. INFO: Application startup complete.

您可以访问127.0.0.1:8000查看您的应用程序。

耶 🎉，你已经成功创建了你的应用程序。 现在让我们部署。

部署步骤。

现在您已经成功创建了您的应用程序，让我们开始部署吧。

安装 Deta Space CLI。

要在 Windows 上安装 Space CLI，请打开 powershell 并粘贴此命令；

 iwr https://get.deta.dev/space-cli.ps1 -useb | iex

对于 Mac PC；

 curl -fsSL https://get.deta.dev/space-cli.sh | sh

登录到细节空间。

成功安装 CLI 后，下一步就是登录 Deta Space。 回到您的项目终端窗口，粘贴以下命令；

 $ space login ? Enter access token >

上面的命令将要求一个“访问令牌”来验证您的 CLI。 在下一步中生成令牌。

生成访问令牌。

要生成访问令牌，请登录您的空间仪表板（在网络上），单击搜索（命令）栏，然后单击“设置”

然后点击“生成令牌”

您将获得一个新令牌，将其复制并保存在安全的地方，因为您将无法再次查看该令牌。 将令牌粘贴到 CLI 提示符中，然后单击 enter。 您应该会看到一条成功消息。

创建一个新的空间项目。

键入以下命令以创建新的 Space 项目。

系统将提示您输入项目名称。 接下来，CLI 将尝试检测目录中的任何现有应用程序。 如果找到，CLI 将显示为这些应用程序生成的配置。

一旦您确认生成的配置，CLI 将在 Builder 中创建一个新项目，并在您的本地目录中生成一个Spacefile文件，其中包含您的应用程序的配置，Deta Space 使用它来了解您的应用程序的外观以及如何运行它. 整个过程应该是这样的；

将您的应用推向太空。

为此，请运行此命令；

此命令将验证您的Spacefile ，然后将所有必要的文件上传到 Deta 的构建管道。

如果由于某种原因你收到一个错误! Failed to push code and create a revision. Please try again! ! Failed to push code and create a revision. Please try again! 确保src指向您拥有main.py和requirements.txt文件的目录，然后再次运行该命令。 您应该会收到一条成功消息

✓ Successfully started your build! ✓ Successfully pushed your Spacefile!

恭喜 🤩，你已经成功部署了你的项目。 要实时查看您的新项目，请转到您的空间仪表板，您会在那里找到您创建的新应用程序，单击该应用程序，它将带您到实时应用程序，您可以在其中复制链接并分享给其他人.

编码愉快😁！



原文:

According to the documentation here,

“FastAPI is a modern, fast (high-performance), web framework for building APIs with Python 3.7+ based on standard Python type hints.”

FastAPI is an easy to learn Python framework which I love best for building my APIs. If you would like to learn more about it, you can visit the documentation. In this article, we’re concerned with how to deploy your FastAPI app on Deta Space so it can be available for use to anyone around the world.

A little about Deta. Deta is a company that offers various services including cloud, Deta Base (Instantly usable database with a feature-rich API.), python and Node.js deployment, all for free.

If you’re familiar with DETA, you would know that they used to be deta.sh (deprecated), but after more than 2 years, they decided to make an upgrade to DETA CLOUD (which is even way cooler) to give developers a free and easy way to get their ideas live on the internet.

I remember then, I used to deploy my fastAPI app on DETA MICRO (deprecated), but since it’s now deprecated, in this article, you’ll learn with how to deploy your FastAPI app on DETA SPACE so it can be available for use to anyone around the world.

But first things first, let’s create a simple FastAPI app for demonstration purposes.

REQUIREMENTS.

  • Python.
  • Deta Space account (remember, it’s totally free. You can create one here). NOTE: if you have a deta.sh account before, you can still use that to login to Deta Space.

CREATE A SIMPLE APP.

Let’s create a simple FastAPI app.

Create a directory.

Open a new terminal window and create a directory for your app, I’ll name mine fastapi-tutorial, then cd into it.

$ mkdir fastapi-tutorial
$ cd fastapi-tutorial

Create a virtual environment.

This step is optional, but it makes sense. The importance of creating a virtual environment is to isolate your project dependencies from your global dependencies on your PC. For windows;

$ python -m venv my-env
$ my-env\Scripts\activate

Or on a mac PC.

$ python3 -m venv my-env
$ Source my-env\bin\activate

Install FastApi.

Now, you’ll need to install fastapi and uvicorn. Uvicorn is an ASGI server, for production.

(my-env) $ pip install fastapi
(my-env) $ pip install "uvicorn[standard]"

Create a simple route.

Create a file main.py and add the following code;

from fastapi import FastAPI

app = FastAPI()


@app.get("/")
def read_root():
    return {"message": "Deploying FastApi to Deta Space"}

Create a requirements.txt file.

Create a requirements.txt file, this will contain all your dependencies so Deta will know which dependency to install for your app.

(my-env) $ pip freeze > requirements.txt

Open the file, it should look something like this;

fastapi==0.89.1
uvicorn==0.20.0

Run the app.

Now, run the app to be sure everything works fine.

(my-env) $ uvicorn main:app --reload

INFO:     Uvicorn running on http://127.0.0.1:8000 (Press CTRL+C to quit)
INFO:     Started reloader process [28720]
INFO:     Started server process [28722]
INFO:     Waiting for application startup.
INFO:     Application startup complete.

You can visit 127.0.0.1:8000 to see your app.

Yay 🎉, you’ve successfully created your app. Now let’s deploy.

DEPLOYMENT STEPS.

Now that you’ve successfully created your app, let’s get to deployment.

Install Deta Space CLI.

To install the Space CLI on windows, open powershell and paste this command;

iwr https://get.deta.dev/space-cli.ps1 -useb | iex

And for a Mac PC;

curl -fsSL https://get.deta.dev/space-cli.sh | sh

Login to Deta Space.

Once you’ve successfully installed the CLI, the next thing is to login to Deta Space. Back to your project terminal window, paste the following command;

$ space login
? Enter access token >

The above command will ask for an “access token” to authenticate your CLI. Generate the token in the next step.

Generate an access token.

To generate an access token, login to your space dashboard (on the web), click on the search (command) bar, then click “Settings”

Then click “Generate token”

You’ll get a new token, copy it and save it somewhere safe, because you won't be able to view the token again. Paste the token in the CLI prompt, then click enter. You should see a success message.

Create a new Space project.

Type the following command to create a new Space project.

You will be prompted to enter a name for your project. Next, the CLI will try to detect any existing applications in your directoriy. If found, the CLI will show a generated config for those applications.

Once you confirm the generated config, the CLI will create a new project in Builder and generate a Spacefile file in your local directory, which contains the configuration of your app, used by Deta Space to understand what your app looks like and how to run it. The whole process should look something like this;

Push your app to space.

To do this run this command;

This command will validate your Spacefile, then upload all the necessary files to Deta’s build pipeline.

If for some reason you,re getting an error ! Failed to push code and create a revision. Please try again!, open your Spacefile (usually at the root directory of your project), it should look like this;

# Spacefile Docs: https://go.deta.dev/docs/spacefile/v0
v: 0
micros:
  - name: FastAPI
    src: .
    engine: python3.9
    primary: true

Make sure the src is pointing to the directory where you have your main.py and requirements.txt files, then run the command again. You should get a success message

✓ Successfully started your build!
✓ Successfully pushed your Spacefile!

Congratulations 🤩, you’ve successfully deployed your project. To see your new project live, go to your space dashboard, you’ll find the new app you created there, click on the app, and it’ll take you to the live app, where you can copy the link and share to others.

Happy coding 😁!

Bình Thạnh là một quận nội thành thuộc thành phố Hồ Chí Minh, Việt Nam. Quận Bình Thạnh là điểm đầu mối giữa quốc lộ 1A và 13, nơi có bến xe Miền Đông, là cửa ngõ con tuyến đường sắt Bắc – Nam vào thành phố này. item.title: 周末阅读 | Weekend Reading
item.description:
大家好！

再次返回另一部分。 那一周去哪儿了？ 我希望你的一周充满了合并和关闭 Jira 的。

本周我们发布了很多版本，从 Vanilla Extract 到 VSCode。 开发人员不断推出下一个 GPT 应用程序，因此还有更多。 哦，当然，正如人们可能预料的那样，向公众发布 Twitter 算法这件小事就一发不可收拾了。

我们跳进去好吗？

香草提取物添加了级联层支持。 我还没有玩过这种 CSS 样式，但它看起来很有趣！

这是设计系统的游戏规则改变者。 为此向@mrm008大喊大叫。

作用域层： vanilla-extract.style/documentation/…

全局层： vanilla-extract.style/documentation/…

简洁的介绍页面，可能值得一看，因为它听起来很令人兴奋。

就是这样。 这感觉就像我在整个职业生涯中一直在等待建立的课程，我很高兴你终于看到它。你可以在网站上注册更新。 反应.gg

感觉已经开始迁移到DrizzleORM了。

它可以做很多巧妙的事情，我只希望它有更多的支持，这样我就可以在工作中深入研究它。

你知道 React 正在开发反应式编译器吗？ 这是我们如何*思考* React 数据流以及编译器的角色应该是什么的显着转变。 你自然而然地编写逻辑，默认情况下 UI 总是一致的，以后不需要重构代码。 如果你像我一样一直在玩 next 13，你可能会遇到一些问题。 好消息是我们正在积极处理其中的大部分内容！我想我会快速回顾一下线程中的一些反馈，供后续（更长的消息）使用。↓

本地开发者beta.nextjs.org/docs/configuri…… twitter.com/i/web/status/1……

新的一周，新的 GPT 应用程序。 考虑到很多 Twitter TS 明星似乎都在避免阵营中，实际上分裂得令人惊讶。

你是否使用 TypeScript 枚举？ 为什么？ 我知道这是有分歧的——我想向我的课程参加者展示一些不同的意见。

这周我没时间看，但Ben 录制了与 Dan 的流媒体会话，他们在微框架中构建了 RSC，并深入了解了内部工作原理。 听起来超级酷，迫不及待想看。 虽然4小时！ 直到下一次。

一旦停赛，运动员将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的停赛被解除。

取消暂停后，运动者将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，运动员的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果运动者未被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以通过以下方式举报运动员：

降低运动员的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

不屈不挠的运动员将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Hello folks!

Back again with another instalment. Where did that week go? I hope your week was filled with merges and closed Jira's.

We had quite a few releases this week, from Vanilla Extract to VSCode. Developers keep churning out the next GPT app, so plenty more of them too. And Twitter didn't disappoint for another week with some long but interesting threads.

Oh, and of course, the small matter of releasing the Twitter algorithm to the public where all hell broke loose, as probably expected.

Let's jump in shall we?

VSCode released their March update this week, nothing overly exciting, but the TS/JS switch case completions is nice.

Vanilla Extract added cascade layer support. I still haven't played with this style of CSS yet, but it looks interesting!

Vanilla Extract just shipped support for cascade layers with support for both locally and globally scoped layers.

This is a game changer for design systems. Massive shout out to @mrm008 for this one.

Scoped layers: vanilla-extract.style/documentation/…

Global layers: vanilla-extract.style/documentation/…

Tyler, of ui dot dev, announced a new React course is on the way. Neat looking intro page, might be worth a watch as it sounds exciting.

That's it. This feels like the course I've been waiting my whole career to build, and I'm excited for you to finally see it.You can signup for updates on the site. Beta launch will begin early May.

react.gg

Theo laments the database performance of Prisma in his latest video. It feels like the migration has begun to DrizzleORM.

Josh shares some more coolness with the :has() selector. There's so much neat stuff this can do, I only wish it had more support so I could dig into it at work.

🌠 The `:has` pseudoclass is so friggin' powerful.Tobias Ahlin shares how to select an item *before* a given element, like selecting the first box before the circle.

tobiasahlin.com/blog/previous-…

Dan reminds us they're still working on the React Forget compiler, the promise-land of not needing fiddly useEffects and array dependencies.

did you know React is working on a reactivity compiler? 👀now that i have your interest, i invite you to read this part of our last blog post in case you missed it.

it was a notable shift in how we *think* about the React data flow and what the compiler’s role should be. twitter.com/dan_abramov/st…

@bholmesdev @astrodotbuild @solid_js if you’re curious about our philosophy and approach to update perf, this update on our compiler research explains the north star. you write the logic naturally, the UI is always consistent by default, and there is no need to restructure the code later. https://t.co/Km4nVp5zev https://t.co/eVyjJynibA

If you've been playing with next 13 as I have, you might have ran into a few problems here and there. Lee posted a thread with the most common questions/gotchas and what's coming next!

@dan_abramov Thanks for posting this 😁 Lots of great feedback in this thread. The good news is that we're actively working on most of these!I figured I'd quickly recap some of the feedback in the thread for those following along (longer message).↓

Local dev beta.nextjs.org/docs/configuri…… twitter.com/i/web/status/1…

A new week, a new GPT app. DomainsGPT got a fair amount of eyes this week it seemed 👀

Wes was asking whether people prefer enums over as const. Surprisingly well split actually given a lot of Twitter TS stars seem to be in the avoid camp.

Do you use TypeScript enums or not? Why? I know it's divisive - I want to show my course takers some differing opinions.

I haven't had time to watch it this week but Ben recorded a stream session with Dan where they built out RSC's in a micro framework and go in depth on the inner workings. Sounded super cool, can't wait to watch. 4 hours though! 😅

Seb's having a week off from This Week In React this week, so that's all we've got! Until next time.

Lorenzo Zarantonello - Mar 30

原文:

item.title: Docker 命令 - 初学者（第一部分） | Docker commands - beginner (part1)
item.description:
在 Ubuntu 上安装 Docker

在 Windows 上安装 Docker

如果没有下载图片，它会自动下载。 图像的版本将是最新的。

命令：docker run ubuntu cat /etc/ release

下载特定图像（版本）

要下载 ubuntu 的特定映像，例如 ubuntu 18.04，命令将是：

特定镜像的官方版本标签，用户可以在hub.docker.com/上找到。

列出容器： docker ps ， docker ps -a

停止容器：docker stop name_of_container

删除容器：docker rm name_of_container

拉取图像： docker pull name_of_image（例如：docker pull image ubuntu）

列出 DOCKER 主机上的图像： docker images、docker images -a（所有图像）

删除图像： docker rmi name_of_image

重要提示：要从 Docker 主机中删除图像，用户需要检查它未运行的容器。 用户需要停止并删除所有相关容器才能删除图像。

例子：

当你想删除 ubuntu-latest 镜像时，你会收到部分错误消息“dependencies -container 6a17ef9”，用户需要先删除容器 (6a17..) 的依赖项，然后再删除镜像。 如果您有更多与此类似的错误，但容器数量不同，请再次执行所有步骤 a)、b)、c)（可选）。

a) Remove container: docker rm 6a17efd a)移除容器： docker rm 6a17efd

b) Remove image: docker rmi ubuntu-latest b)移除镜像： docker rmi ubuntu-latest

c) List images to check that is deleted: docker images c)列出镜像以检查是否已删除： docker images



原文:

Install Docker on Ubuntu

Install Docker on Windows

Install Docker on AWS (EC2 Instance)

RUN — the docker run command is used to run a container from an image.

If the image is not downloaded, it will automatically download it. The version of image it will be the latest.

COMMAND: docker run ubuntu cat /etc/release

DOWNLOAD SPECIFIC IMAGE (VERSION)

To download specific image of ubuntu, for example ubuntu 18.04, the command will be:

docker run ubuntu:18.04 /etc/release

/etc/release — will show other details about the image

Numbers of version of ubuntu 18.04 in the Docker world is called TAGS. Official versions of tags for specific image, user can find on the hub.docker.com/ .

LIST CONTAINERS: docker ps , docker ps -a

STOP CONTAINER: docker stop name_of_container

REMOVE CONTAINER: docker rm name_of_container

PULL IMAGE: docker pull name_of_image (example: docker pull image ubuntu)

LIST IMAGES ON DOCKER HOST: docker images, docker images -a (all images)

REMOVE IMAGES: docker rmi name_of_image

IMPORTANT: To remove images from Docker host, user need to check that container it’s not running. User need to STOP AND DELETE all dependent containers to be able to delete an image.

Example:

When you want to delete image ubuntu-latest and you get part of error message is “dependencies -container 6a17ef9 “ , user need first remove dependencies of container (6a17..) and then remove image. If you have more errors similar to this, but with different number of container, do it all steps again a), b), c) (optional) .

a) Remove container: docker rm 6a17efd

b) Remove image: docker rmi ubuntu-latest

c) List images to check that is deleted: docker images

item.title: 如何像专业人士一样粉刷厨柜 | How to Paint Your Kitchen Cabinets Like a Pro
item.description:
您是否希望在不破坏资金的情况下让您的厨房焕然一新？ 粉刷厨柜是一种经济实惠且有效的改变厨房外观的方法。 然而，粉刷橱柜可能是一项艰巨的任务，尤其是如果您以前从未做过。

在这篇文章中，我们将指导您完成六个简单的步骤，像专业人士一样为您的厨柜上漆。 按照这些步骤，您很快就会拥有一个漂亮而专业的厨房！

像专业人士一样粉刷厨柜的 6 个步骤

以下六个步骤将帮助您像专业人士一样粉刷厨柜：

步骤 #1：拆下柜门和五金件

在开始为厨柜上漆之前，您需要拆除所有柜门和五金件。 使用螺丝刀从每扇门上卸下铰链和把手。 卸下所有门和硬件后，用肥皂和水彻底清洁它们。

第 2 步：清洁和打磨橱柜

要清洁和打磨机柜，请使用温水和 TSP（磷酸三钠）的混合物彻底清洁机柜。 然后，用砂纸打磨橱柜，直到它们光滑且没有任何颠簸或瑕疵。

第 3 步：装柜

清洁和打磨后，是时候给橱柜涂底漆了。 使用画笔或滚筒在橱柜上涂一层底漆。 这将有助于油漆更好地附着，并确保橱柜表面光滑均匀。 在进行下一步之前，确保让底漆彻底干燥。

步骤#4：油漆橱柜

底漆干燥后，您就可以开始给橱柜上漆了。 选择专为橱柜使用而配制的优质油漆。 使用画笔或滚筒将油漆涂成薄而均匀的涂层。 在涂下一层之前，让每一层油漆完全干燥。

步骤#5：重新安装硬件和门

油漆完全干燥后，您可以重新安装硬件和门。 使用螺丝刀将铰链和把手重新连接到每扇门上。 在拧紧螺丝之前，确保门正确对齐。

第 6 步：享受您美丽的新厨柜！

恭喜，您已经像专业人士一样成功地粉刷了您的厨柜！ 现在，是时候坐下来享受美丽的新厨房了。 为您自己动手省钱而感到自豪，而且您的厨房看起来就像是由温哥华的专业画家粉刷过的。 如需任何进一步的帮助，您可以随时与我们联系。

结论

粉刷厨柜似乎是一项艰巨的任务，但并非必须如此。 遵循这六个简单的步骤，您很快就会拥有一个漂亮而专业的厨房。 记得拆下橱柜门和五金件，清洁和打磨橱柜，给橱柜打底漆，给橱柜上漆，然后重新安装五金件和门。 如果您的绘画项目需要帮助，请随时联系我们，温哥华的专业画家。 我们很乐意为您提供帮助！ 立即访问AZ Painting 。



原文:

Are you looking to refresh your kitchen without breaking the bank? Painting your kitchen cabinets is a budget-friendly and effective way to transform the look of your kitchen. However, painting your cabinets can be a daunting task, especially if you have never done it before.

In this post, we will guide you through six simple steps to paint your kitchen cabinets like a pro. Follow these steps and you will have a beautiful and professional-looking kitchen in no time!

6 Steps to Paint Your Kitchen Cabinets Like A Pro

The following six steps will help you paint your kitchen cabinets like a pro:

Step #1: Remove Cabinet Doors and Hardware

Before you begin painting your kitchen cabinets, you need to remove all the cabinet doors and hardware. Use a screwdriver to remove the hinges and handles from each door. Once you have removed all the doors and hardware, clean them thoroughly using soap and water.

Step #2: Clean and Sand the Cabinets

To clean and sand the cabinets use a mixture of warm water and TSP (trisodium phosphate) to clean the cabinets thoroughly. Then, use sandpaper to sand the cabinets until they are smooth and free of any bumps or imperfections.

Step #3: Prime the Cabinets

After cleaning and sanding, it's time to prime the cabinets. Apply a coat of primer to the cabinets using a paintbrush or a roller. This will help the paint adhere better and ensure that the cabinets have a smooth and even finish. Make sure to let the primer dry thoroughly before proceeding to the next step.

Step #4: Paint the Cabinets

Once the primer is dry, you can start painting the cabinets. Select a top-quality paint that is specially formulated for use on cabinets. Use a paintbrush or a roller to apply the paint in thin, even coats. Let each coat of paint get dried completely before you apply the next one.

Step #5: Reinstall the Hardware and Doors

After the paint has dried completely, you can reinstall the hardware and doors. Use a screwdriver to reattach the hinges and handles to each door. Make sure that the doors are aligned properly before tightening the screws.

Step #6: Enjoy Your Beautiful New Kitchen Cabinets!

Congratulations, you have successfully painted your kitchen cabinets like a pro! Now, it's time to sit back and enjoy your beautiful new kitchen. Take pride in the fact that you saved money by doing it yourself, and that your kitchen looks like it was painted by professional painters in Vancouver. For any further help, you can freely contact us.

Conclusion

Painting your kitchen cabinets may seem like a daunting task, but it doesn't have to be. Follow these six simple steps and you'll have a beautiful and professional-looking kitchen in no time. Remember to remove the cabinet doors and hardware, clean and sand the cabinets, prime the cabinets, paint the cabinets, and then reinstall the hardware and doors. If you need help with your painting project, don't hesitate to contact us, professional painters in Vancouver. We'll be happy to assist you! Visit AZ Painting today.

关于如何在 Laravel 中测试任务调度，我想说说我自己的经验。 说到测试，可以进行某种时间旅行，但我不确定。

我的做法是在命令中编写任务，我们知道命令是 PHP 的常规类，我们可以为其编写测试以确保其正常工作。 但是对于任务调度，为了确保任务将在特定时间间隔运行，我们可能必须信任服务器的核心任务调度程序:)

因此，请正确测试您自己的任务并对其进行测试，并相信每个时间间隔都会继续运行您的任务。

原文:

Amir Haghi

Posted on Apr 1

I want to say my own experience about how to test task scheduling in Laravel. There is a task scheduler in Laravel that you can write your task in a callback function and it is completely explained in the docs. When it comes to testing it is possible to work with some sort of time traveling but I'm not sure about that.

The way that I do is that I write my task in a command and as we know a command is a regular class of PHP and we can write tests for that to be sure that is working correctly. But for task scheduling, in order to be sure that the task will be run on the specific time intervals, we have to trust to the core task schedulers of servers maybe :)

So test your own task correct and test it and trust to on to run your task is every interval.

Tell your opinion please.

Nikola Stojiljkovic - Feb 5

原文:

你是否喜欢一些小众且无广告的软件？如果是的话，我这边有一些给你推荐的。

1.取色工具——ColorPix

ColorPix是一款简单的取色工具，可以随时捕捉屏幕上的颜色，并显示其RGB值和十六进制代码。ColorPix还支持放大镜和颜色锁定功能，让你取色更准确、更方便。

2.备忘录——Sticky Notes

Sticky Notes是一款模拟便利贴的备忘录软件，可以在桌面上创建多个彩色的便签，记录你的日程、任务、想法等。Sticky Notes还支持字体设置、背景颜色、透明度调节等功能，让你的备忘录更个性化、更美观。

3.微信空号检测——燃精灵

燃精灵是一款在云端进行微信空号检测的软件，只需要把待测的号码段以TXT文本上传至平台，等待检测结果就行。搜索燃精灵，注册推荐码填6666就可以使用了。

4.轻量浏览器——Via

Via是一款轻量级的网络浏览器，只有500多KB，但功能强大，支持多标签、夜间模式、广告拦截、自定义主题等。Via还支持自定义搜索引擎、手势操作、书签管理等，让你的网络浏览更快捷、更舒适。

5.网络收藏——Pocket

Pocket是一款网络收藏工具，可以让你保存网页、文章、视频等内容，方便以后阅读或观看。Pocket还支持离线阅读、标签管理、推荐功能等，让你的网络收藏更有序、更有趣。

这些就是我给你推荐的一些小众且无广告的软件，有兴趣的可以自行搜索下载，有别的想要分享的软件可以在评论区留言。

item.title: 短跑运动员 vs 马拉松运动员 | Sprinters vs marathonians
item.description:
马拉特波夫

发表于 4 月 1 日

太神奇了，但开发者不一样！ 我并不是指不同的编程语言或堆栈。 我的意思是编程哲学。

有些程序员，我们称他们为“短跑运动员”，编写的代码可以正常工作。 这是唯一的内部验收标准。

其他人，让我们称他们为“马拉松运动员”，编写代码不仅是为了让它工作，而且是为了能够维护它多年。

主要区别在于“短跑运动员”不编写测试，实际上也不关心编码风格。 乍一看，长期专注于维护的程序员以最好的方式做事。 但是，它们的成本通常更高，并且需要花费更多的时间进行编码。

谁知道？ 也许对于短期、低预算的项目，我们应该表现得更像“短跑运动员”？

一旦暂停，altesack 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，altesack 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，altesack 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 altesack 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 altesack：

降低 altesack 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 altesack 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Marat Latypov

Posted on Apr 1

It's amazing, but developers are different! And I don’t mean different programming languages or stack. I mean a programming philosophy.

Some programmers, let’s call them “sprinters”, write code that just works. This is the only internal acceptance criterion.

Others , let’s call them “marathonians”,write code not only to make it work, but also to be able to maintain it for years.

The main difference is that “sprinters” don't write tests and actually don't care about coding style. The “marathonians” are obsessed with coding style and tests writing to the point where sparks fly, keeping in the mind SOLID and other dev tricks.

The “marathonians” don't like “sprinters” - they write sloppy code, "just to get it done".

The “sprinters” don't like the second - they are doing tons of useless work, “art for the sake of art”.

At first sight, long-term maintenance-focused programmers do things the best way. However, they often come at a higher cost and spend more time on coding.

Who knows? Maybe for short-term, low-budget projects, we should act more like "sprinters"?

Peter Kelvin Torver - Mar 31

item.title: 大数字 | BigNumbers
item.description:
哈桑·哈比卜·塔希尔

发表于 4 月 1 日

具体来说，uint256 的最大值为：

在Javascript 的数字数据类型中，上限要小得多。
 具体来说，Javascript 可以容纳的数值类型的最大值就是(9007199254740991)；

如果该数字大于 Javascript 的最大数值（这是绝对可能的），那么会发生什么？ 因此，我们必须使用一种称为 BigNumber 的特殊类型。

用于与以太坊节点交互的库——ethers.js 和 web3.js——都支持 BigNumbers。

一旦暂停，hassanhabibtahir 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 hassanhabibtahir：

降低 hassanhabibtahir 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 hassanhabibtahir 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

HassanHabibTahir

Posted on Apr 1

BigNumber is an object which safely allows mathematical operations on numbers of any magnitude uint256 has a range from 0 to (2^256) - 1. Therefore, the maximum number that can be held by the uint256 datatype is astronomically large. Specifically, the maximum value for uint256 is:

115792089237316195423570985008687907853269984665640564039457584007913129639935

In Javascript's number data type has a significantly smaller upper limit.
Specifically, the maximum value of a numeric type that Javascript can hold is just (9007199254740991);

If that number is greater than Javascript's maximum numeric value, which is quite definitely possible, then what happens? Javascript can not support that. Therefore, we have to use a special type called a BigNumber.

ibraries used for interacting with Ethereum nodes - ethers.js and web3.js - both come with support for BigNumbers.

BigNumber is a custom class library, written in Javascript, that introduces it's own functions for mathematical functions - add, sub, mul, div, etc. BigNumber has a significantly larger capacity for numbers than what Javascript can natively support.

item.title: 优化 kubernetes 上的 spark | Optimize spark on kubernetes
item.description:
这是我关于 Kubernetes 上的 Spark 的第二篇文章。 我想分享我在降低云中 Spark 计算成本方面的经验，这可能很昂贵，但可以降低 60-70%。 我正在使用 Spark 3.3.1 版。

如果您从 iPython notebook 以客户端模式运行您的研究，建议使用动态分配。 此配置允许您仅在计算期间创建执行程序 pod，之后执行程序将停止。

 spark.dynamicAllocation.enabled true spark.dynamicAllocation.shuffleTracking.enabled true spark.dynamicAllocation.shuffleTracking.timeout 120 spark.dynamicAllocation.minExecutors 0 spark.dynamicAllocation.maxExecutors 10

为执行者使用 spot 节点可以显着降低成本（比按需节点便宜 60-90%）。 创建一个spot节点组，需要给它打上标签，比如spark:spot。 但是，对于驱动程序，仍应使用按需节点。

如果你是在客户端模式下运行，设置如下配置

spark.kubernetes.executor.node.selector.spark spot # here you label k,v in my case k=spark, v=node

如果您使用的是 Spark Operator，请使用以下配置设置：

 spec: driver: nodeSelector: - key1: value1 - key2: value2 executor: nodeSelector: - key1: value1 - key2: value2

使用 SSD 卷挂载到执行程序。 如上所述，在 Spot 节点中断的情况下保持执行程序临时结果。 为此，最好使用 SSD 卷挂载，它可以加速 Spark 保存在磁盘上的临时文件的读写。 您可以使用以下配置设置：

 spark.kubernetes.executor.volumes.persistentVolumeClaim.data.options.claimName OnDemand spark.kubernetes.executor.volumes.persistentVolumeClaim.data.options.storageClass gp # your cloud ssd storage class spark.kubernetes.executor.volumes.persistentVolumeClaim.data.options.sizeLimit 100Gi spark.kubernetes.executor.volumes.persistentVolumeClaim.data.mount.path /data spark.kubernetes.executor.volumes.persistentVolumeClaim.data.mount.readOnly false

这些是“Learning Spark”中推荐的默认值：

 spark.shuffle.file.buffer 1m spark.file.transferTo false spark.shuffle.unsafe.file.output.buffer 1m spark.io.compression.lz4.blockSize 512k

总之，通过执行上述步骤，您可以显着降低在云中运行 Spark 计算的成本。 动态分配、为执行器使用 spot 节点和 SSD 卷装载可以将成本降低高达 60-90%。 此外，按照“Learning Spark”中的建议使用默认值有助于优化性能。 请记住，在进行任何更改时始终优先考虑用户的需求和满意度，并在实施之前彻底测试任何配置。 通过这样做，您可以为您的用户提供有用且愉快的体验，同时还具有成本效益。

资源：

spot.io/blog/how-to-run-spark-on-kubern… spot.io/blog/how-to-run-spark-on-kubern…


aws.amazon.com/blogs/compute/running-co… aws.amazon.com/blogs/compute/running-co…
spark.apache.org/docs/latest/running-on… spark.apache.org/docs/latest/running-on…
www.oreilly.com/library/view/learning-s… www.oreilly.com/library/view/learning-s…


原文:

This is my second post about Spark on Kubernetes. I wanted to share my experience with reducing the costs of Spark computation in clouds, which can be expensive, but can be decreased by 60-70%. I am using Spark version 3.3.1.

'1. If you are running your research in client mode from iPython notebook, it is recommended to use dynamic allocation. This configuration allows you to create an executor pod only during compute time, after which the executor stops.

spark.dynamicAllocation.enabled                     true
spark.dynamicAllocation.shuffleTracking.enabled     true
spark.dynamicAllocation.shuffleTracking.timeout     120
spark.dynamicAllocation.minExecutors                0
spark.dynamicAllocation.maxExecutors                10

'2. Using spot nodes for executors significantly reduce costs (60-90% cheaper than on-demand nodes). To create a spot node group, you need to label it, for example, spark: spot. However, for driver still on-demand nodes should be used.

If you are running in client mode, set the following configuration

spark.kubernetes.executor.node.selector.spark      spot  # here you label k,v in my case k=spark, v=node

If you are using Spark Operator, use the following configuration settings:

spec:
  driver:
    nodeSelector:
      - key1: value1
      - key2: value2
  executor:
    nodeSelector:
      - key1: value1
      - key2: value2

P.S use volume mount from next point to keep executors temp results is case of spot node interruption

'3. Use SSD volume mount to executors. As mentioned above to keep executor temp results in case of spot node interruption. For this purpose, it is best to use an SSD volume mount, which accelerates the write and read of temp files that Spark saves on disk. You can use the following configuration settings:

spark.kubernetes.executor.volumes.persistentVolumeClaim.data.options.claimName    OnDemand
spark.kubernetes.executor.volumes.persistentVolumeClaim.data.options.storageClass    gp # your cloud ssd storage class
spark.kubernetes.executor.volumes.persistentVolumeClaim.data.options.sizeLimit    100Gi
spark.kubernetes.executor.volumes.persistentVolumeClaim.data.mount.path    /data
spark.kubernetes.executor.volumes.persistentVolumeClaim.data.mount.readOnly    false

'4. These are the recommended default values from "Learning Spark":

spark.shuffle.file.buffer                           1m
spark.file.transferTo                               false
spark.shuffle.unsafe.file.output.buffer             1m
spark.io.compression.lz4.blockSize                  512k

In conclusion, by following the above steps, you can significantly reduce the cost of running Spark computations in the cloud. Dynamic allocation, using spot nodes for executors, and SSD volume mounts can reduce costs by up to 60-90%. Additionally, using default values as recommended in "Learning Spark" can help optimize performance. Remember to always prioritize the needs and satisfaction of the user when making any changes and to thoroughly test any configurations before implementing them. By doing so, you can provide a useful and enjoyable experience for your users while also being cost-effective.

Recources:

spot.io/blog/how-to-run-spark-on-kubern…


aws.amazon.com/blogs/compute/running-co…
spark.apache.org/docs/latest/running-on…
www.oreilly.com/library/view/learning-s…
item.author: akoshel

item.title: 顶级 React 数据获取库 | Top React Data Fetching Libraries
item.description:
使用 XMLHttpRequest 和 Fetch API 在 React 中获取数据很好，但需要大量样板代码。 首选使用数据获取库，因为以下解决方案将使用大量惊人的内置功能简化项目中数据获取的逻辑。

热门图书馆

在服务器端，它使用本机 NodeJS http模块，而在浏览器上，它使用 XMLHttpRequests。

它与后端无关，因此您可以将 React Query 与任何异步数据获取客户端一起使用。

使用它来获取、缓存和修改应用程序数据，同时自动更新您的 UI。

数据抓取

他们通常还需要对该数据进行更新，将这些更新发送到服务器，并使客户端上的缓存数据与服务器上的数据保持同步。 由于需要实现当今应用程序中使用的其他行为，这变得更加复杂：

  • 跟踪加载状态以显示 UI 微调器
  • 避免对相同数据的重复请求
  • 乐观的更新让 UI 感觉更快
  • 在用户与 UI 交互时管理缓存生命周期

共同特征

  • 传输和协议无关（REST、GraphQL 等）
  • 缓存、重新获取、轮询、实时
  • 分页、加载更多、无限滚动
  • 请求和响应拦截
  • 焦点重新验证，网络恢复
  • 性能优化
  • 取消请求

原文:

Fetching data in React using XMLHttpRequest and Fetch API is fine but require a lot of boilerplate codes. Using a data fetching library is preferred as following solutions will simplify the logic of data fetching in your project with tons of amazing built-in features.

Popular libraries

Axios (99k ⭐) -> A promise-based isomorphic HTTP client which can run in the browser and NodeJS with the same codebase. On the server-side it uses the native NodeJS http module, while on the browser it uses XMLHttpRequests.

React Query (33k ⭐) -> A library handles fetching, caching, background updates and stale data out of the box with zero-configuration. It is backend agnostic so you can use React Query with literally any asynchronous data fetching client.

SWR (26k ⭐) -> A React Hooks library for data fetching, created by Next.js team, lightweight, and backend agnostic.

Apollo Client (18k ⭐) -> A comprehensive state management library for JavaScript that enables you to manage both local and remote data with GraphQL. Use it to fetch, cache, and modify application data, all while automatically updating your UI.

Relay (17k ⭐) -> The production-ready GraphQL client for React, developed by Facebook, was designed to be performant from the ground up, built upon locally declaring data dependencies for components.

urql (8k ⭐) -> A highly customizable and versatile GraphQL client with which you add on features like normalized caching as you grow.

RTK Query (9k ⭐) -> An optional addon included in the Redux Toolkit package, and its functionality is built on top of the other APIs in Redux Toolkit.

Data fetching

Web applications normally need to fetch data from a server in order to display it. They also usually need to make updates to that data, send those updates to the server, and keep the cached data on the client in sync with the data on the server. This is made more complicated by the need to implement other behaviors used in today's applications:

  • Tracking loading state in order to show UI spinners
  • Avoiding duplicate requests for the same data
  • Optimistic updates to make the UI feel faster
  • Managing cache lifetimes as the user interacts with the UI

Common features

  • Transport and protocol agnostic (REST, GraphQL, etc)
  • Caching, refetching, polling, realtime
  • Pagination, load more, infinite scrolling
  • Requests and responses interception
  • Revalidation on focus, network recovery
  • Performance optimizations
  • Request cancellation

Mihovil Ilakovac - Mar 16
